mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and inflation indicates robust consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs rate hikes without triggering recession, supporting positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 60-day returns in >60% of observations during tightening periods across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which translate into higher near-term corporate earnings expectations and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive PAYEMS revisions over a 4-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units indicates a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity market reactions over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods where HOUST drops below 1400.0 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rapidly rising policy rates creates a stagflationary financial environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined high-CPI and rising-rate regime yields positive returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% during a post-shock recovery phase confirms sustained aggregate demand, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% coincides with negative 60-day returns in >55% of quarterly observations during recovery regimes over a 10-year sample.
